Executive Summary

This security assessment identified 2422 potential security issues on the target system. Critical vulnerabilities require immediate attention as they pose significant risk to the organization. High and medium severity issues should be addressed in order of priority. Risk Distribution: - Critical: 61 issues - High: 1051 issues - Medium: 185 issues - Low: 1125 issues

Vulnerabilities

Command Injection

Severity: CRITICAL URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-includes/js/jquery/jquery.min.js?ver=3.6.0 Parameter: ver

Description: Command injection allows arbitrary command execution

Payload:

; ls -la

Evidence: Command output indicator found: uid=

Remediation: Avoid executing system commands with user input

Command Injection

Severity: CRITICAL URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-includes/js/jquery/jquery.min.js?ver=3.6.0 Parameter: ver

Description: Command injection allows arbitrary command execution

Payload:

& ls -la

Evidence: Command output indicator found: uid=

Remediation: Avoid executing system commands with user input

Business Logic - Race Condition

Severity: CRITICAL URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/rev-slider/js/extensions/extensionsrevolution.extension.carousel.min.js

Description: Application vulnerable to race condition attacks

Remediation: Implement proper locking mechanisms and idempotency

Business Logic - Negative Quantity

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js Parameter: quantity

Description: Application accepts negative quantity values

Evidence: Negative quantity accepted: -1

Remediation: Implement positive integer validation for quantities

File Upload - Path Traversal

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: File upload vulnerable to path traversal attacks

Evidence: Path traversal filename accepted: ../../../shell.php

Remediation: Sanitize filenames, use basename(), restrict upload directory

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: shell.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.jpg.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - Polyglot File Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: Application does not scan file content for embedded code

Evidence: Polyglot file (image + code) accepted

Remediation: Implement file content scanning, re-encode images

File Upload - Double Extension Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: Server may execute files with double extensions

Evidence: Double extension file accepted: file.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate complete filename, not just last extension

File Upload - SVG XSS

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/desirable-documents

Description: SVG uploads can be exploited for XSS attacks

Evidence: SVG file with embedded JavaScript accepted

Remediation: Sanitize SVG files, serve with Content-Disposition: attachment

File Upload - Path Traversal

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: File upload vulnerable to path traversal attacks

Evidence: Path traversal filename accepted: ../../../shell.php

Remediation: Sanitize filenames, use basename(), restrict upload directory

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: shell.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.jpg.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - Polyglot File Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: Application does not scan file content for embedded code

Evidence: Polyglot file (image + code) accepted

Remediation: Implement file content scanning, re-encode images

File Upload - Double Extension Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: Server may execute files with double extensions

Evidence: Double extension file accepted: file.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate complete filename, not just last extension

File Upload - SVG XSS

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/essential-documents

Description: SVG uploads can be exploited for XSS attacks

Evidence: SVG file with embedded JavaScript accepted

Remediation: Sanitize SVG files, serve with Content-Disposition: attachment

File Upload - Path Traversal

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: File upload vulnerable to path traversal attacks

Evidence: Path traversal filename accepted: ../../../shell.php

Remediation: Sanitize filenames, use basename(), restrict upload directory

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: shell.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.jpg.php

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - MIME Type Bypass

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: File upload validation can be bypassed with MIME type manipulation

Evidence: Dangerous file accepted with misleading MIME type: image.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate actual file content, not just MIME type or extension

File Upload - Polyglot File Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: Application does not scan file content for embedded code

Evidence: Polyglot file (image + code) accepted

Remediation: Implement file content scanning, re-encode images

File Upload - Double Extension Accepted

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: Server may execute files with double extensions

Evidence: Double extension file accepted: file.php.jpg

Remediation: Validate complete filename, not just last extension

File Upload - SVG XSS

Severity: HIGH URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: SVG uploads can be exploited for XSS attacks

Evidence: SVG file with embedded JavaScript accepted

Remediation: Sanitize SVG files, serve with Content-Disposition: attachment

Severity: MEDIUM URL: https://www.svvit.org/b-e-in-mechanical-engineering

test

Remediation: Validate and sanitize XML input, use XML schema validation

Severity: MEDIUM URL: https://www.svvit.org/naac-documents

Description: Application lacks proper file size limits

Evidence: Large file upload accepted (tested with 10240KB)

Remediation: Implement reasonable file size limits (e.g., 5MB for images)

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js

Description: Clickjacking protection missing

Evidence: Missing header: X-Frame-Options

Remediation: Add X-Frame-Options header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js

Description: MIME sniffing protection missing

Evidence: Missing header: X-Content-Type-Options

Remediation: Add X-Content-Type-Options header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js

Description: HSTS not enforced

Evidence: Missing header: Strict-Transport-Security

Remediation: Add Strict-Transport-Security header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js

Description: CSP not implemented

Evidence: Missing header: Content-Security-Policy

Remediation: Add Content-Security-Policy header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/wp-content/themes/vvit/res/javascript/parallax.js

Description: XSS protection header missing

Evidence: Missing header: X-XSS-Protection

Remediation: Add X-XSS-Protection header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/b-e-in-mechanical-engineering

Description: Clickjacking protection missing

Evidence: Missing header: X-Frame-Options

Remediation: Add X-Frame-Options header to all responses

Security Misconfiguration

Severity: LOW URL: https://www.svvit.org/b-e-in-mechanical-engineering
